steps.title: Identify the y-intercept on the graph
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept is the point where the line crosses the $y$-axis, which occurs when $x = 0$.

Looking at the graph, trace vertically from $x = 0$ to where the line intersects.

The line crosses the $y$-axis at $y = -7$, giving the point $(0, -7)$.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
